test.observation: All 184 tests passed across 12 files, including 9 notification tests. Note: server package has no test script; tests run directly via bun.

typecheck.command: cd packages/server && bunx tsc --noEmit && cd packages/web && bunx tsc --noEmit
typecheck.observation: Both server and web packages pass TypeScript check with no errors

lint.observation: Checked 69 files in 30ms. No fixes applied.

blockingIssues.description: Hold expiration email (booking-hold-expired template) is never sent when a hold actually expires. The sendHoldExpirationEmail function exists and is tested in isolation, but releaseExpiredHoldsAndReturnCount() and the expired-hold check in confirmExistingBooking both call releaseCapacity with reason='expired' but neither sends a hold expiration email notification.
files: packages/server/src/features/bookings/capacity-consume.service.ts:165 - releaseCapacity called with reason='expired' but no email sent
packages/server/src/features/citizen/citizen-portal.service.ts:380 - releaseExpiredHoldsAndReturnCount releases stale holds but never sends emails
blockingIssues.suggestedFix: Integrate sendHoldExpirationEmail call into both releaseExpiredHoldsAndReturnCount() and the expired-hold check in confirmExistingBooking(), passing the booking data (procedure name, slot datetime, applicant name/email) so the email can be rendered and sent.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Fix the typecheck command. Current: 'typecheck: bunx tsc --noEmit' (no project specified, fails). Correct: 'typecheck: cd server && bunx tsc --noEmit' or use 'tsc:server' script from package.json.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Command 'bunx tsc --noEmit' without -p flag doesn't find tsconfig.json. package.json has 'tsc:server: bun --cwd packages/server tsc --noEmit'.
